Empires & Puzzles Charlotta Review: The Prima Donna of Pain

Is Charlotta's opera performance worth the ticket price? We review this Slow Fire hero who forces enemies to dance to her tune (literally). Find out if her 1500 damage finale is enough to keep her relevant in today's fast-paced meta.
Charlotta Review: She Sings, She Dances, She Destroys Your Mana
Charlotta: The Diva Who Forces You to Dance (Whether You Want To Or Not)
challenge event. Released back in late 2024, she was a harbinger of the "1100 Power" era, a simpler time when we thought that was a high number. As a

mana hero, she fits the classic "high risk, high reward" archetype—if she charges, she can sing an aria that ends the battle; if she doesn't, she's just a well-dressed spectator. She brings the unique "Dance" mechanic to the table, forcing enemies to perform the "Ballad of Condemnation" which, spoiler alert, isn't a fun dance.

Visually, Charlotta is a showstopper. She channels a distinct "Queen of Hearts meets Opera Diva" aesthetic. She’s not your typical slender elven warrior; she has character, presence, and arguably the most scrutinized fingers in the game's history (seriously, the community had
about those hands). She looks like she’s about to hit a high C that will shatter your phone screen. It’s a bold, colorful design that stands out in a sea of grimdark warriors.

When Charlotta debuted, her stats were groundbreaking, being the first to crack the 1100 Power ceiling. Fast forward to 2026, and the landscape has shifted. Modern heroes are pushing 1260+ Power with attack stats in the 1300s. Charlotta’s
are now considered average to slightly below average for a top-tier legendary. Her
holds up better, but she is no longer the stat monster she once was. She relies heavily on her family bonus and passives to compete with the modern stat creep.

After 3 turns (or if cleansed/overwritten by another dance), the dancer takes

. In a raid, 12 tiles can feel like an eternity. However, if she fires, she effectively removes 3 enemies from the game—they can't gain mana efficiently, they can't heal effectively, and they are on a countdown to a massive damage spike.
With a Level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent modern troop), you can shave a tile off, but she remains a slow hero. She essentially demands to be played in

The -50% healing is significant against modern healers who rely on boosted health, effectively halving their output.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Opera family and her specific passives are what keep her playable despite the slow speed.
The Opera family bonus provides raw stats (Attack, Defense, HP). While nice, it's a passive boost that doesn't fundamentally change her gameplay, but it helps her survive long enough to fire.
This is her survival tool. Gaining
whenever a status ailment expires or is cleansed is massive. In a meta heavy with ailments, she can passively regenerate huge amounts of health, making her surprisingly tanky.
She cannot be hit with Attack Down, ensuring her 450% hit always lands with full force.
fits her kit perfectly. Since she needs time to charge, passive healing helps mitigate the damage she takes while waiting for tiles.
Upon release, Charlotta was a "Rush War Goddess." She was the shiny new toy that made Rush tournaments a nightmare. She was also notable for normalizing the 1100+ power level.

The game has gotten faster and stats have gotten bigger. Slow heroes without "game-winning" immediate effects struggle in standard raids. While her damage is still lethal, the prevalence of "Toon" passives (75% resist) and monks/clerics makes landing her dance less reliable.
formats and effective in PvE events (like Towers) where mana control is key. In standard high-level Diamond Arena raids, she is often too slow and too risky compared to Faster, Modern alternatives.
As a Sorcerer, Charlotta has access to the Delay talent, which can further disrupt enemy mana.
She is Slow. Her primary goal is to survive long enough to cast
. If she casts, she likely wins her flank. Extra defense and health capitalize on her passive healing.

Maximizes the 450% initial slam. Good if you are using her primarily for the upfront damage in events.

path is the way to go. A dead Charlotta deals zero damage. Keep her alive, let her passive heal her, and wait for the 12 tiles.

The combination of 450% Hit-3 and 1500 delayed damage is lethal.
Passive healing on ailment expiry makes her very resilient against DoT-heavy teams.
She is an absolute monster in Very Fast wars and tournaments.
The biggest drawback. In standard play, the match is often decided before she fires.
Toons, Monks, and Clerics can resist the dance. Gazelle and other "Dance" heroes can overwrite her effect.
Unlike some slow heroes who hit all, she leaves two enemies untouched.
This is her home. At Very Fast speed, she is a top-tier threat.
Her high damage and mana control are great for boss waves (if you can manage the speed).
Great for nuking bosses with the 1500 fixed damage.
. Place Charlotta next to Ludwig (or another mana booster). Ludwig forces her to charge, allowing you to unleash her slow skill repeatedly.
. Combine her -50% mana gen with minion mana control to ensure the enemy never charges.
Other "Dance" heroes. If Gazelle dances, Charlotta's dance cannot be applied (or is removed/replaced depending on the specific interaction order).
The passive resistance to ailments (75%) makes Charlotta's dance very unreliable against full Toon teams.
A taunter can absorb the hit, protecting the other two targets from the dance.

If you pull Charlotta early, congratulations! She will carry you through almost all map stages and lower-level raids. Her raw stats will be higher than anything else you have. Park her in the middle of your defense or on a flank and watch her work. Don't worry too much about her speed early on; matches last longer in lower arenas.
Charlotta is a luxury tool for your

depth. She isn't worth Limit Breaking (LB2) unless you are absolutely desperate for a Red Rush tank/flank. With the arrival of 1260+ power heroes, her stats are lagging. Save your Alpha Aethers for heroes with more versatile speeds or modern stat lines.
The community's reaction to Charlotta was... mixed.
Players couldn't help but notice her hands on the card art. One player noted,
"Those fingers are a bit chubby though man... like decently large sized fingers."
Many players compared her unfavorably to
"After Von Rothy, Charlotta feels under cooked... Von Rothbart's actually probably a stronger hero."
The argument was that Rothbart hits all and is Average speed (after setup), whereas Charlotta is stuck at Slow and Hit-3.
"Too bad she’s slow… I needed a good red project."
Charlotta is like a grand piano: heavy, hard to move, but capable of making beautiful music when positioned correctly. In the era of hyper-fast ninjas and goblins, she feels a bit clunky. But in

, she takes center stage and reminds everyone why you don't mess with the Prima Donna. If you have her, use her for those specific modes, but don't expect her to keep up with the newest speedsters in daily raiding.
who hits like a truck but moves like a glacier. Keep her for the niche, but don't force her into your main defense.
A comprehensive review of Charlotta, the 5-star Fire Sorcerer from the Secrets of the Opera event. We analyze her stats, special skill, and viability in the current meta.
